topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion in the otherwise quiet afternoon. My stomach clenched instantly, a familiar, cold knot tightening beneath my ribs. It was a request from Mark. Mark, the architect of my early awkwardness, the one whose sneers still echoed in the hollow spaces of my memory. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. A wave of nausea washed over me, making the air feel thick and hard to draw into my lungs. Why now? What could he possibly want? I scrolled through my contacts, my gaze darting back to the little blue circle bearing his name, a strange mix of dread and a flicker of something unwelcome—curiosity—stirring within me. I hesitated, the silence in my room amplifying the frantic drumming of my pulse.

[story 2]

I stared at the profile picture—a slightly too-confident smirk that used to send a jolt of pure anxiety through me. It was him. The memory of his shadow looming over me in the hallway, the sharp, cutting words he’d thrown my way, flooded my senses, making my palms instantly slick. A deep sigh escaped my lips, a sound ragged and shallow. I didn't want this. I wanted to delete the notification, to erase the digital tether to that unpleasant chapter of my life. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscle strain in my neck. Every instinct screamed at me to block, to ignore, but a small, unwelcome part of my mind lingered, weighing the potential consequences against the sheer discomfort of complete avoidance.

[story 3]

The sound of the phone vibrating felt like a physical blow. I picked it up slowly, my hand shaking so badly I almost dropped it. Seeing the name pop up—Mark—sent a rush of icy adrenaline through my system. My breath hitched, catching painfully in my throat. I looked around the room, suddenly hyper-aware of every shadow, every creak in the floorboards. This felt like bait, a deliberate provocation. My eyes scanned the message, reading the simple invitation as if it were a trap laid with surgical precision. A slow, deliberate shake of my head signaled a decision, a silent refusal forming in my chest, heavy and resistant. I placed the phone face down, focusing intently on the dust motes dancing in the sunlight, trying desperately to anchor myself against the rising tide of unease.
